topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an irritating, high-pitched whine, mirroring the frantic buzzing in Maya’s ears. She reread the accusation, the printed words blurring into an illegible smear on the page. Every time she glanced at the stack of annotated papers, a cold knot tightened in her stomach, pulling her down. Her hands, usually steady enough for complex calculus problems, trembled slightly as she gripped the edge of the desk. *It can’t be true. I didn't copy anything.* The silence of the study carrel felt enormous, amplifying the rapid thump of her own heart against her ribs. She rubbed her temples, the pressure behind her eyes building until a dull ache radiated across her forehead. She needed air, needed distance, but the thought of leaving the building felt like admitting defeat.

[story 2]

“Again? Just one more time?” Liam asked, his voice a low, strained whisper that barely cut through the background chatter of the common room. He kept running a hand through his already disheveled hair, his gaze darting nervously toward the main entrance. Every time the administrative assistant approached, Liam felt a sudden, sharp tightening in his chest, like a vise squeezing his lungs. He meticulously straightened a loose thread on his sweater, an unconscious attempt to impose order on the chaos churning inside him. *What do I even say?* The words felt thick and clumsy on his tongue. He stared at the worn textbook open on the table, the ink seeming to mock him with its flawless, copied structure. He wanted to bolt, to disappear into the anonymity of the crowded hallway, but his feet felt rooted to the spot.

[story 3]

The air in the small dorm room felt too thick, heavy, pressing down on Alex’s chest until breathing became a conscious, shallow effort. He paced the length of the threadbare rug three times, the rhythmic scuff of his worn sneakers sounding deafening in the quiet space. He kept catching his reflection in the darkened windowpane—a pale, drawn-out expression that seemed far too intense for the mundane reality of a university assignment. He picked up his phone, intending to call a friend, but his thumb hovered over the contact list. The impending meeting felt like a physical weight, anchoring him to the floor. Each passing minute stretched into an agonizing eternity, punctuated only by the frantic drumming inside his ears, a relentless internal alarm signaling imminent collapse.
